title: delegation — compliance evidence
description: Impersonation is the module's compliance-heavy surface. Every impersonation session is evidence for SOC 2 CC6.1 + CC6.7 (privileged access audit) + PCI-DSS 8.5 (no shared / group / generic accounts — impersonation is the exception, and the audit stream is the compensating control). Delegation is compliance-lighter — a User handing a role to another User is a routine business event captured under standard activity logging.

controls.evidence: Every impersonation_session row is a first-class evidence artefact: impersonator identity (via FK + snapshot fields), impersonated identity, target application + tenant, exact start + end times, reason (min 40 chars), support ticket reference (when captured), read-only flag, end trigger. 7-year hot retention preserves evidence beyond the SOC 2 Type II audit window (typically 12 months trailing + entire cumulative history).

The impersonation Sanctum PAT is scoped to abilities of the impersonated User (never inherits the impersonator's platform_admin abilities); the JWT `app` claim binds the PAT to a single target application so it cannot be replayed against other applications. Every write during the session is audited with both the impersonated User (as actor) + the impersonator PlatformUser (as `impersonated_by_platform_user_id`).

Impersonation is gated behind (a) the `platform.impersonate` permission on the platform_admin guard, (b) the `impersonation` entitlement on the PlatformUser row, (c) MFA-mandatory via the platform.mfa_required middleware, (d) the target-tenant + target-application checks. Every attempt (including refusals) is logged via ImpersonationAttemptRefused. Access-review is done by security team via the `impersonation:audit-report` command.
Health probes flag stuck-active-impersonation sessions + token-scrub lag. Metrics feed dashboards; PagerDuty is paged when stuck-active count > 0.

The LockdownImpersonationForTenant listener responds to compliance::TenantSecurityIncidentDeclared events — force-terminates all active impersonation on the affected tenant + refuses new attempts until the incident is closed. Automated incident response.

controls.principle: do not use group / shared / generic accounts / passwords
controls.evidence: Impersonation is Stackra's LEGITIMATE exception to shared-account use — a support agent operating under a customer's identity for troubleshooting. The compensating controls are: (a) MFA-mandatory on the initiating platform_admin session, (b) short TTL (max 60 minutes default), (c) reason-required (40 chars minimum), (d) mandatory customer notice (cannot-opt-out impersonation.session_started notification), (e) full audit trail preserved 7 years. Every impersonation session is documented as a compliance evidence artefact.

controls.principle: implement audit trails to reconstruct events
controls.evidence: Every impersonation session + every action taken during it is captured in HasAudit's audit stream with correlation IDs linking actor (impersonated user) to operator (impersonator PlatformUser).

The lawful basis for processing impersonation records + delegation records is the legitimate interest of the tenant (audit / accountability) and the platform operator (security / support quality). Tenants are informed via the DPA + the cannot-opt-out impersonation notice.

controls.principle: information provided when data is collected from the data subject
controls.evidence: The cannot-opt-out ImpersonationSessionStartedNotification is the Art. 13 notice: it informs the impersonated User that their account is being processed by an Stackra staff member + provides the identity + basis + retention information.

controls.principle: right to erasure — with Art. 17(3)(e) legal claims defence exception
controls.evidence: When a User exercises Art. 17 erasure, the impersonation_sessions row IS cascaded (target CASCADE on impersonated_user_id) but the HasAudit audit stream is retained under Art. 17(3)(e) — the audit is necessary for the establishment + defence of legal claims (accountability audit for regulator inspection). The impersonator's own actions on their own PlatformUser row are separate + fall under the platform-user retention policy.

The impersonator's identity in the audit is minimised (display_name + department snapshot; no PII beyond what the audit reader needs). The impersonation-scoped PAT never inherits the impersonator's platform_admin abilities (least-privilege by construction).
session_token_hash is SHA-256 hashed; raw token is never persisted. Hash is NULLED 30 days after session end (residue removal). MFA-mandatory on operator authentication.

wave_2_hook.description: When a healthcare tenant adopts, session.metadata.compliance_regime opens the door for HIPAA-adjacent audit enhancements: 6-year retention (HIPAA §164.316(b)(2)) instead of 7-year, encryption-at-rest requirement stamps on the audit stream, break-glass authorization pathway for emergency impersonation without customer consent (documented in HIPAA §164.312(a)(2)(ii)). Wave 2 open door.
wave_2_hook.current_gap: The compliance_regime hook exists as a JSON key but is not consumed by any consumer today. When the first healthcare tenant lands, additional Wave 2 work: (a) IHE + HL7 audit-stream enhancements, (b) BAA sign-off flow for the impersonating PlatformUser, (c) 6-year retention branch.
